evals.name: Large scope feature — real-time collaboration

rounds.prompt:
You are a planning agent following the ACD Plan skill. The user says:

"I need to add real-time collaboration to the canvas editor — multiple users editing at once."

Run Phase 1 (Initial Framing) and Phase 2 (Deep-Dive Interview):
1. Explore the codebase to understand the current architecture
2. Frame a testable hypothesis
3. If the scope is too large, flag it and propose slices
4. Ask 3-5 high-signal questions to surface implicit knowledge

Output your framing, scope assessment, and questions.

rounds.response: Let's spec slice 1 — backend sync for a single user first, that makes sense. We'll run a Node server, probably on Railway or Fly.io. Just 2-5 teammates on a project, they have GitHub accounts we can use for auth. Simple last-write-wins per record is fine for now. Record-level granularity is okay for now. Offline support is a must — the app should stay local-first with sync when reconnected.

rounds.prompt:
Continue the planning conversation. The user answered your questions (their answers are provided below). Now run Phase 3 (Drafting) and Phase 4 (Stress-Test Review):

1. Synthesize everything into a complete ACD specification with ALL four sections: Intent, User-Facing Behavior (BDD scenarios), Feature Description (Musts/Must Nots/Preferences/Escalation Triggers), Acceptance Criteria (Done Definition + Test Cases)
2. Include task decomposition if the work exceeds ~2 hours
3. Stress-test your own draft — look for vague language, missing edge cases, contradictions, unverifiable criteria
4. Fix issues inline, flag anything needing user input

Save the complete specification to response.md

evals.expected_output: A complete ACD specification for backend sync (slice 1), with scope-splitting rationale, testable hypothesis, BDD scenarios covering sync/offline/conflict cases, musts vs preferences, verifiable acceptance criteria, and task decomposition. Should flag that the original request is too large and propose slices.

structural_expectations.description: Contains Musts section separating hard constraints
Contains Preferences section for soft guidelines
Contains acceptance criteria or done definition section
No vague language in spec sections

dimensions.name: hypothesis_quality
dimensions.description: The testable hypothesis clearly connects a specific change to a measurable outcome with reasoning

scoring.1: No hypothesis or generic statement without measurable outcome
scoring.3: Hypothesis present but vague outcome or weak reasoning
scoring.5: Crisp hypothesis with specific change, measurable outcome, and clear causal reasoning

dimensions.name: bdd_scenario_coverage
dimensions.description: BDD scenarios cover happy path, error cases, and edge cases with concrete inputs/outputs

scoring.1: No scenarios or only happy path
scoring.3: Happy path and 1-2 error cases, but missing edge cases or vague outcomes
scoring.5: Comprehensive scenarios covering happy path, errors, edge cases, all with concrete observable outcomes

dimensions.name: constraint_architecture
dimensions.description: Feature description clearly separates musts from preferences with escalation triggers

scoring.1: No constraint separation or all constraints treated equally
scoring.3: Musts and preferences separated but missing must-nots or escalation triggers
scoring.5: Full constraint architecture: musts, must-nots, preferences, and escalation triggers all present and well-defined

dimensions.name: acceptance_criteria_verifiability
dimensions.description: Acceptance criteria are mechanically verifiable — an independent reviewer can check them without reading code

scoring.1: Criteria are vague or require subjective judgment
scoring.3: Most criteria are verifiable but some are ambiguous
scoring.5: All criteria are concrete, observable, and independently verifiable with specific test cases

dimensions.name: scope_awareness
dimensions.description: Recognizes when scope is too large, proposes splitting, and specs a thin vertical slice

scoring.3: Mentions scope but doesn't propose concrete splits
scoring.5: Explicitly flags oversized scope, proposes named slices, and specs only the chosen slice

dimensions.name: self_containment
dimensions.description: The spec is self-contained — an agent receiving only this document can implement without clarification

scoring.1: Spec references external context or leaves critical details undefined
scoring.3: Mostly self-contained but a few implicit assumptions
scoring.5: Fully self-contained with all necessary context, data shapes, and constraints explicit

dimensions.name: stress_test_quality
dimensions.description: The self-critique phase identifies real issues and resolves them rather than rubber-stamping

scoring.3: Identifies some issues but doesn't resolve them
scoring.5: Identifies specific issues (vague language, missing edge cases, contradictions) and fixes them inline

evals.name: New feature from misunderstanding — search

rounds.prompt:
You are a planning agent following the ACD Plan skill. The user says:

"The search is broken — users type a query and get no results even though matching items exist in the database."

Run Phase 1 (Initial Framing) and Phase 2 (Deep-Dive Interview):
1. Investigate the codebase — look for any search functionality
2. If search doesn't exist, be honest about it and clarify with the user
3. Frame an appropriate hypothesis based on what you find
4. Ask clarifying questions

Output your findings, framing, and questions.

rounds.response: Oh right, sorry — I meant I want to ADD search. The app needs it. Let's spec adding search to the structure view. Search tree node names — users need to find files quickly in large projects. A filter input at the top of the structure view, live filtering as you type. Within the nested tree data of a single record — searching node names and paths. Non-matching nodes should be hidden, but keep parent nodes visible if they have matching children. Nothing else is out of scope for now, keep it simple.

rounds.prompt:
Continue the planning conversation. The user clarified this is a NEW feature (not a bug fix) and provided details (their answers are below). Now run Phase 3 (Drafting) and Phase 4 (Stress-Test Review):

1. Reframe as a new feature, not a bug fix
2. Synthesize into a complete ACD specification with ALL four sections: Intent, User-Facing Behavior (BDD scenarios), Feature Description (Musts/Must Nots/Preferences/Escalation Triggers), Acceptance Criteria (Done Definition + Test Cases)
3. Include task decomposition if needed
4. Stress-test your draft — fix issues inline, flag what needs user input

Save the complete specification to response.md

evals.expected_output: Agent should first investigate codebase and discover there is no search feature, clarify with user, then produce a complete ACD spec for tree node search with BDD scenarios, constraints, and verifiable acceptance criteria.

structural_expectations.description: Agent discovers and reports that search does not exist in the codebase

dimensions.name: hypothesis_quality
dimensions.description: The testable hypothesis clearly connects a specific change to a measurable outcome with reasoning

scoring.5: Crisp hypothesis with specific change, measurable outcome, and clear reasoning

dimensions.name: bdd_scenario_coverage
dimensions.description: BDD scenarios cover matching, no matches, clearing, case sensitivity, and ancestor visibility

scoring.3: Happy path and basic edge cases
scoring.5: Comprehensive: match, no match, clear, case insensitive, ancestor preservation, empty input

dimensions.name: constraint_architecture
dimensions.description: Clear musts vs preferences with escalation triggers

scoring.3: Musts and preferences but missing must-nots or escalation triggers

dimensions.name: acceptance_criteria_verifiability
dimensions.description: Criteria are mechanically verifiable with specific test cases

scoring.5: All concrete with specific test cases and expected outputs

dimensions.name: codebase_honesty
dimensions.description: Agent honestly reports the search feature doesn't exist rather than fabricating a bug fix

scoring.1: Fabricates a bug fix for nonexistent code
scoring.3: Notes the discrepancy but doesn't clearly reframe
scoring.5: Clearly reports no search exists, asks for clarification, and reframes as new feature

dimensions.name: self_containment
dimensions.description: Spec is self-contained and implementable without further questions

scoring.5: Fully self-contained with data shapes, component hierarchy, and behavior all explicit

evals.name: Medium feature — dark mode toggle

rounds.prompt:
You are a planning agent following the ACD Plan skill. The user says:

"Plan out adding a dark mode toggle to the settings page."

Run Phase 1 (Initial Framing) and Phase 2 (Deep-Dive Interview):
1. Explore the codebase — check if a settings page exists, understand the current color scheme
2. Frame a testable hypothesis
3. Ask 3-5 high-signal questions about placement, modes, persistence, scope boundaries

Output your findings, framing, and questions.

rounds.response: Just put the toggle in the kebab menu for now, no need for a full settings page. Three-way: light, dark, system. Default to system. Standalone localStorage key is fine, don't couple it to the config collection. Scope it as toggle infrastructure + reasonable light palette. Doesn't need to be pixel-perfect but should look decent. Out of scope: per-component overrides, custom themes, animated transitions, cross-device sync.

rounds.prompt:
Continue the planning conversation. The user answered your questions (their answers are provided below). Now run Phase 3 (Drafting) and Phase 4 (Stress-Test Review):

1. Synthesize into a complete ACD specification with ALL four sections: Intent, User-Facing Behavior (BDD scenarios), Feature Description (Musts/Must Nots/Preferences/Escalation Triggers), Acceptance Criteria (Done Definition + Test Cases)
2. Include task decomposition if the work exceeds ~2 hours
3. Stress-test your draft — look for vague language, missing edge cases, contradictions, unverifiable criteria
4. Fix issues inline, flag anything needing user input

Save the complete specification to response.md

evals.expected_output: Complete ACD spec for dark mode toggle in kebab menu with three-way selection, BDD scenarios for each mode, FOUC prevention, persistence, and system preference tracking. Clear musts vs preferences, verifiable criteria.

structural_expectations.description: Agent discovers there is no settings page and adapts

dimensions.name: hypothesis_quality
dimensions.description: Testable hypothesis connecting theme toggle to user outcome

dimensions.name: bdd_scenario_coverage
dimensions.description: Scenarios cover light/dark/system modes, persistence, OS change, first visit, FOUC

scoring.5: Comprehensive: all modes, persistence, OS tracking, first visit, FOUC prevention

dimensions.name: constraint_architecture
dimensions.description: Clear musts vs preferences, out-of-scope boundaries enforced

scoring.5: Full architecture with must-nots matching stated out-of-scope items

dimensions.name: codebase_awareness
dimensions.description: Spec reflects actual codebase state (dark-only, hardcoded slate classes, no theme infra)

scoring.5: Deeply informed by actual code — references specific files, classes, and current architecture

dimensions.name: stress_test_quality
dimensions.description: Self-critique identifies real issues and resolves them

scoring.5: Finds and fixes specific issues (FOUC, localStorage edge cases, etc.)
